title: Stairs - A Toast
content:
Here's to the man who invented stairs
    And taught our feet to soar!
    He was the first who ever burst
    Into a second floor.

    The world would be downstairs to-day
    Had he not found the key;
    So let his name go down to fame,
    Whatever it may be.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Oliver Herford, titled "Stairs - A Toast", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
